changes: Field uses `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` for serialization control
file_insights.documentation: SHA256 checksum of canonical JSON serialization for corruption detection; absent checksums trigger JSONL replay

patterns_discovered.description: Uses `Option<T>` with `serde(default)` and `skip_serializing_if` to maintain backward compatibility while supporting new fields
Checksum field enables fallback mechanism: if missing or invalid checksum, system replays JSONL log to reconstruct state
patterns_discovered.alignment: Matches documented storage decision requiring meta.json checksums for fault tolerance

patterns_discovered.description: Optional field design allows graceful upgrade path for older meta.json files without checksums

gotchas_discovered.description: Comment references 'absent checksums trigger JSONL replay' but replay logic not in this subtask; upstream code must implement the detection and fallback
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Design assumes sibling code in `read_meta` function checks for None and initiates JSONL replay

gotchas_discovered.description: Field added but no implementation of SHA256 computation or verification; these must be implemented in separate subtask
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Follow-up work needed in serialization paths

gotchas_discovered.description: Checksum stored as String; no validation that it's valid hex encoding or length constraints enforced by type system
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider custom type wrapper in future if validation becomes critical

approach_outcome.description: Successfully integrated checksum field into Meta struct following serde best practices
approach_outcome.design_alignment: Aligns with bop's documented storage model (fs state machine + JSONL event log + meta.json with corruption detection)
approach_outcome.backward_compatibility: Maintained through optional field and serde skip-if-none

recommendations.action: Implement checksum computation during meta.json serialization
recommendations.rationale: Field exists but is never populated; compute SHA256 of canonical JSON (excluding checksum field itself) on write

recommendations.action: Implement checksum verification in read_meta function
recommendations.rationale: Verify computed checksum matches stored value; on mismatch trigger documented JSONL replay fallback

recommendations.action: Add unit tests for serialization/deserialization with and without checksum
recommendations.rationale: Verify backward compatibility (missing checksum deserializes correctly) and forward compatibility (checksum round-trips)

recommendations.action: Document checksum computation algorithm explicitly in code
recommendations.rationale: Comments mention SHA256 but don't specify canonical form; document how JSON is serialized for deterministic hashing

recommendations.action: Consider NewType wrapper for checksum (e.g., `type Checksum = String`)
recommendations.rationale: Provides semantic clarity and future-proofs for validation constraints if needed
